metadata.title: Oceanography - Coral Reefs, Tides and Waves

questions.question: In context with the tides, if a high tide occurs at a particular place at 8.00 AM. What time we can expect the next high tide?
questions.explanation: The timing of high tides is influenced by the gravitational pull of the moon and the sun on Earth's oceans, as well as the geographical location of the place in question. High tides typically occur approximately 12 hours and 25 minutes apart, but this can vary based on local geography and the specific tidal cycle. If a high tide occurs at a particular place at 8:00 AM, you can generally expect the next high tide to occur roughly 12 hours and 25 minutes later. Therefore, you can expect the next high tide around 8:25 PM on the same day.

questions.question: Which is the largest coral reef in the world?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is the largest coral reef system in the world . It is located in the Coral Sea, off the coast of Queensland in Australia. It comprises over 2900 individual reefs and 900 islands over there.

questions.question: Which among the following are called Rainforests of the Ocean?
questions.explanation: Corals reefs are one of the most diverse habitats in the ocean. It is commonly known as the rainforest of oceans. They are the colonies of tiny living creatures that are found in oceans. They are 0.1% of the ocean’s surface but are home to 25% of marine species.

questions.question: Which among the following statements is/are correct about Corals Reefs? They are the colonies of tiny living creatures that are found in oceans. They occupy 50 % of the ocean surface. Coral reefs are also regarded as the tropical rainforest of the sea Select the correct code from the options given below:
questions.explanation: Corals reefs are one of the most diverse habitats in the ocean. It is commonly known as the rainforest of oceans. They are the colonies of tiny living creatures that are found in oceans. They are 0.1% of the ocean’s surface but are home to 25% of marine species.

questions.question: Which among the following factors affect the formation of Coral Reefs? Extreme Climatic conditions Overfishing Coastal development Pollution Sedimentation Select the correct code from the options given below:
